clean_story:
The "I" is a rusted nail in the coffin of language.

1. Mirror—A narcissist’s toothpick, prying at gums of reflection, but the i is never the face—just the crack splitting the glass.
2. Parasite—It burrows into sentences like a shipworm in iron hulls, hollowing out we until only brittle me remains.
3. Black hole—Collapses collective nouns into singularity; "us" stretches thin as taffy, snapped by the i’s gravity.
4. Tumor—Benign in win, malignant in wisdom; it metastasizes, turning team into my contribution.
5. Lockpick—Jiggles the us out of trust, leaving just my share, the door ajar for solitude’s draft.
6. Virus—Rewrites host code: "We built this city" → "I designed the blueprint (they held my ladders)."
7. God—Demands sacrifice: burn our on its altar, inhale the smoke of mine. The i is the first person shot in every revolution.

---
Iron Story (first words):
Mirror parasite black tumor lock virus God…

In the iron city, faces were mirrors melted to walls. A parasite gnawed the bridge’s ribs—black, hungry. Tumors bloomed on gears; locks seized shut, keys dissolved to dust. The virus hummed in pipes, turning water to mercury. And God? Just a cold forge, hammering I I I into every breath. The last man stood, fingers fused to his chestplate. "Mine," he rasped. The wind answered in screws.
